passage_title: HOW DOES THE BIOLOGICAL CLOCK TICK?

sentences.sentence: Our life span is restricted.
sentences.translation: 我们的寿命是有限的。
difficult_points: is restricted 是被动语态，表示寿命受到限制。
life span 指“寿命”，不是泛指生活。

sentences.sentence: Everyone accepts this as 'biologically' obvious.
sentences.translation: 每个人都认为这一点在生物学上显而易见。
difficult_points: accept A as B 表示“认为A是B”。
this 指前一句寿命有限这一事实。

sentences.sentence: 'Nothing lives forever!' However, in this statement we think of artificially produced, technical objects, products which are subjected to natural wear and tear during use.
sentences.translation: “没有什么能永远存在！”不过，说这句话时，我们想到的是人工制造的技术物品——这些产品在使用中会受到自然磨损。

structure.text: which are subjected to natural wear and tear during use

difficult_points: products 是 objects 的同位语，which 从句修饰 products。
be subjected to 表示“受到……影响”；wear and tear 是固定搭配。

sentences.sentence: This leads to the result that at some time or other the object stops working and is unusable ('death' in the biological sense).
sentences.translation: 这会导致这样的结果：物体迟早会停止工作而无法使用（在生物学意义上就是“死亡”）。

structure.text: that at some time or other the object stops working and is unusable

difficult_points: that 从句说明 result 的具体内容；at some time or other 表示“迟早”。
stops working 与 is unusable 并列，描述停止工作的结果。

sentences.sentence: But are the wear and tear and loss of function of technical objects and the death of living organisms really similar or comparable?
sentences.translation: 但是，技术物品的磨损和功能丧失，与生物体的死亡，真的相似或可相比吗？

structure.text: the wear and tear and loss of function of technical objects and the death of living organisms

difficult_points: 疑问句将 are 提前，主语是两个并列名词短语。
similar 与 comparable 是并列表语，分别表示相似和可比较。

sentences.sentence: Our 'dead' products are 'static', closed systems.
sentences.translation: 我们所谓“死去”的产品是静态的、封闭的系统。
difficult_points: dead 和 static 加引号，表示类比意义而非真正生物死亡。
closed systems 是与 static 并列的补语。

sentences.sentence: It is always the basic material which constitutes the object and which, in the natural course of things, is worn down and becomes 'older'.
sentences.translation: 始终是构成物体的基本材料，在自然过程中逐渐磨损并变得“更老”。

structure.text: It is always the basic material
and which, in the natural course of things, is worn down and becomes 'older'
in the natural course of things

difficult_points: It is...which... 是强调句，强调 basic material。
两个 which 从句并列修饰 material；第二个从句中插入了 in the natural course of things。

expressions.text: in the natural course of things

sentences.sentence: Ageing in this case must occur according to the laws of physical chemistry and of thermodynamics.
sentences.translation: 在这种情况下，老化必然遵循物理化学和热力学定律。

structure.text: according to the laws of physical chemistry and of thermodynamics

difficult_points: must occur 表示必然发生；according to 引出所遵循的规律。
两个 of 短语并列修饰 laws。

sentences.sentence: Although the same law holds for a living organism, the result of this law is not inexorable in the same way.
sentences.translation: 虽然同一定律也适用于生物体，但它产生的结果并非以同样不可避免的方式发生。

structure.text: Although the same law holds for a living organism
is not inexorable in the same way

difficult_points: Although 引导让步从句，holds for 表示“适用于”。
inexorable 表示“不可阻挡、不可避免的”，修饰结果发生方式。

sentences.sentence: At least as long as a biological system has the ability to renew itself it could actually become older without ageing; an organism is an open, dynamic system through which new material continuously flows.
sentences.translation: 至少只要生物系统有自我更新的能力，它实际上就可能变老却不发生老化；生物体是一个开放的动态系统，新物质不断流经其中。

structure.text: At least as long as a biological system has the ability to renew itself
could actually become older without ageing
through which new material continuously flows

difficult_points: as long as 引导条件从句，it 指 biological system。
become older without ageing 表示年龄增加但没有功能性老化；through which 修饰 system。

sentences.sentence: Destruction of old material and formation of new material are thus in permanent dynamic equilibrium.
sentences.translation: 因此，旧物质的破坏和新物质的形成处于永久的动态平衡之中。

structure.text: Destruction of old material and formation of new material

difficult_points: 主语由 destruction 和 formation 两个并列动名词构成，谓语用 are。
dynamic equilibrium 指不断变化中仍保持的平衡。

sentences.sentence: The material of which the organism is formed changes continuously.
sentences.translation: 构成生物体的物质在不断变化。

structure.text: The material of which the organism is formed
of which the organism is formed

difficult_points: of which 引导定语从句，表示“由这种物质构成”。
changes 的主语是 material，不是 organism。

sentences.sentence: Thus our bodies continuously exchange old substance for new, just like a spring which more or less maintains its form and movement, but in which the water molecules are always different.
sentences.translation: 因此，我们的身体不断用新物质替换旧物质，就像泉水大致保持形状和流动，却始终由不同的水分子组成。

structure.text: which more or less maintains its form and movement
but in which the water molecules are always different

difficult_points: exchange A for B 表示“用B替换A”，new 后省略了 substance。
两个 which 从句修饰 spring，第二个 in which 指泉水内部。

sentences.sentence: Thus ageing and death should not be seen as inevitable, particularly as the organism possesses many mechanisms for repair.
sentences.translation: 因此，不应把衰老和死亡视为不可避免，尤其是因为生物体拥有许多修复机制。

structure.text: particularly as the organism possesses many mechanisms for repair

difficult_points: should not be seen as 是被动表达，意为“不应被看作”。
particularly as 引出加强理由，as 在此表示“因为”。

sentences.sentence: It is not, in principle, necessary for a biological system to age and die.
sentences.translation: 原则上，生物系统并非必须衰老和死亡。

structure.text: for a biological system to age and die

difficult_points: it 是形式主语，真正主语是 for a biological system to age and die。
in principle 是插入状语，表示“原则上”。

sentences.sentence: Nevertheless, a restricted life span, ageing, and then death are basic characteristics of life.
sentences.translation: 然而，有限的寿命、衰老以及随后的死亡，是生命的基本特征。

structure.text: a restricted life span, ageing, and then death

difficult_points: 三个并列成分共同作主语，谓语用 are。
then 表示死亡发生在衰老之后。

sentences.sentence: The reason for this is easy to recognise: in nature, the existent organisms either adapt or are regularly replaced by new types.
sentences.translation: 这一点的原因很容易理解：在自然界，现存生物要么适应环境，要么定期被新类型取代。

structure.text: either adapt or are regularly replaced by new types

difficult_points: either...or... 连接 adapt 与 are replaced 两种结果。
are replaced by 是被动语态，new types 是替代者。

sentences.sentence: Because of changes in the genetic material (mutations) these have new characteristics and in the course of their individual lives they are tested for optimal or better adaptation to the environmental conditions.
sentences.translation: 由于遗传物质发生变化（突变），这些生物具有新的特征；在各自的生命历程中，它们会接受检验，看是否能更好地适应环境条件。

structure.text: Because of changes in the genetic material (mutations)
in the course of their individual lives
for optimal or better adaptation to the environmental conditions

difficult_points: these 指前句的新类型；Because of 后接名词短语，mutations 是同位解释。
are tested for 表示“接受检验以判断是否具备……适应性”。

sentences.sentence: Immortality would disturb this system—it needs room for new and better life.
sentences.translation: 永生会扰乱这一系统——系统需要为更新、更好的生命留出空间。

structure.text: room for new and better life

difficult_points: would disturb 表示假设结果；破折号后 it 指 this system。
room for... 是“为……留出空间”的抽象用法。

sentences.sentence: This is the basic problem of evolution.
sentences.translation: 这就是进化的基本问题。
difficult_points: This 指前句关于永生与更新空间的矛盾。
of evolution 表示 problem 所属的领域。

sentences.sentence: Every organism has a life span which is highly characteristic.
sentences.translation: 每种生物都有其高度独特的寿命。
difficult_points: which 修饰 life span，说明寿命具有该物种特征。
Every 后接单数 organism，谓语用 has。

expressions.meaning_zh: 高度独特的；有鲜明特征的

sentences.sentence: There are striking differences in life span between different species, but within one species the parameter is relatively constant.
sentences.translation: 不同物种之间的寿命存在显著差异，但在同一物种内，这一指标相对稳定。

structure.text: striking differences in life span between different species
but within one species the parameter is relatively constant

difficult_points: There are 结构的真正主语是 striking differences。
parameter 指前文 life span，within one species 表示在同一物种内部。

sentences.sentence: For example, the average duration of human life has hardly changed in thousands of years.
sentences.translation: 例如，数千年来人类平均寿命几乎没有变化。

structure.text: the average duration of human life

difficult_points: has hardly changed 是现在完成时，说明变化从过去延续到现在。
hardly 表示“几乎不”，不是完全没有。

sentences.sentence: Although more and more people attain an advanced age as a result of developments in medical care and better nutrition, the characteristic upper limit for most remains 80 years.
sentences.translation: 虽然由于医疗发展和营养改善，越来越多人达到高龄，但大多数人的典型寿命上限仍是80岁。

structure.text: Although more and more people attain an advanced age as a result of developments in medical care and better nutrition
the characteristic upper limit for most

difficult_points: Although 让步从句中的 as a result of 短语说明达到高龄的原因。
upper limit for most 中 most 指大多数人，remains 后接数值作补语。

sentences.sentence: A further argument against the simple wear and tear theory is the observation that the time within which organisms age lies between a few days (even a few hours for unicellular organisms) and several thousand years, as with mammoth trees.
sentences.translation: 反对简单磨损理论的另一个论据是：生物衰老所需的时间从几天（单细胞生物甚至只需几小时）到几千年不等，巨型树木就是如此。

structure.text: A further argument against the simple wear and tear theory
the observation that the time within which organisms age lies between a few days (even a few hours for unicellular organisms) and several thousand years

difficult_points: that 从句说明 observation 的内容；within which 修饰 time。
lies between A and B 表示范围从A到B，括号补充单细胞生物的极端情况。

sentences.sentence: If a life span is a genetically determined biological characteristic, it is logically necessary to propose the existence of an internal clock, which in some way measures and controls the ageing process and which finally determines death as the last step in a fixed programme.
sentences.translation: 如果寿命是由基因决定的生物特征，那么从逻辑上说，就必须假设存在一个内部时钟，它以某种方式测量并控制衰老过程，最终把死亡确定为固定程序的最后一步。

structure.text: If a life span is a genetically determined biological characteristic
to propose the existence of an internal clock
which in some way measures and controls the ageing process
and which finally determines death as the last step in a fixed programme

difficult_points: it is necessary to propose 是形式主语结构，真正主语是 to propose...。
两个 which 从句并列修饰 internal clock，分别说明其功能。

sentences.sentence: Like the life span, the metabolic rate has for different organisms a fixed mathematical relationship to the body mass.
sentences.translation: 和寿命一样，不同生物的代谢率与体重之间存在固定的数学关系。

structure.text: a fixed mathematical relationship to the body mass

difficult_points: Like the life span 是比较状语，说明代谢率与寿命相似。
has 的宾语被放在句末，for different organisms 修饰关系适用范围。

sentences.sentence: In comparison to the life span this relationship is 'inverted': the larger the organism the lower its metabolic rate.
sentences.translation: 与寿命相比，这种关系是“倒置的”：生物体越大，其代谢率越低。

structure.text: In comparison to the life span
the larger the organism the lower its metabolic rate

difficult_points: the larger..., the lower... 是“越……越……”比较结构。
in comparison to 表示比较对象，不是因果关系。

sentences.sentence: Again this relationship is valid not only for birds, but also, similarly on average within the systematic unit, for all other organisms (plants, animals, unicellular organisms).
sentences.translation: 同样，这一关系不仅适用于鸟类，也大体类似地适用于分类单元内的所有其他生物（植物、动物和单细胞生物）。

structure.text: not only for birds, but also, similarly on average within the systematic unit, for all other organisms (plants, animals, unicellular organisms)
similarly on average within the systematic unit

difficult_points: not only...but also... 连接两个适用范围。
similarly on average within the systematic unit 是插入语，限定“平均而言、在同一分类单元内”。

sentences.sentence: Animals which behave 'frugally' with energy become particularly old, for example, crocodiles and tortoises.
sentences.translation: 节省使用能量的动物会活得特别长，例如鳄鱼和乌龟。
difficult_points: which 引导定语从句修饰 animals；frugally 用引号表示节俭使用能量的比喻。
become old 在此表示“变得长寿/活得久”。

sentences.sentence: Parrots and birds of prey are often held chained up.
sentences.translation: 鹦鹉和猛禽常常被拴链圈养。
difficult_points: are held chained up 是被动结构，chained up 作宾语补语。
birds of prey 是“猛禽”的固定表达。

sentences.sentence: Thus they are not able to 'experience life' and so they attain a high life span in captivity.
sentences.translation: 因此，它们无法“体验生活”，所以在圈养环境中反而拥有较长寿命。

structure.text: and so they attain a high life span in captivity

difficult_points: be able to 后接动词原形；experience life 加引号表示充分活动的比喻。
and so 表示结果，in captivity 指在圈养状态下。

sentences.sentence: Animals which save energy by hibernation or lethargy (e.g. bats or hedgehogs) live much longer than those which are always active.
sentences.translation: 通过冬眠或蛰伏节省能量的动物（如蝙蝠或刺猬），比总是活跃的动物寿命长得多。

structure.text: Animals which save energy by hibernation or lethargy
which save energy by hibernation or lethargy
live much longer than those which are always active

difficult_points: 两个 which 从句分别修饰节能动物和 those；those 指另一类动物。
by hibernation or lethargy 说明节省能量的方式。

sentences.sentence: The metabolic rate of mice can be reduced by a very low consumption of food (hunger diet).
sentences.translation: 通过极少量进食（饥饿饮食），老鼠的代谢率可以降低。

structure.text: by a very low consumption of food

difficult_points: can be reduced by 是情态动词加被动语态，by 引出降低代谢率的手段。
hunger diet 是对 very low consumption of food 的同位解释。

sentences.sentence: They then may live twice as long as their well-fed comrades.
sentences.translation: 这样一来，它们的寿命可能是食物充足的同伴的两倍。

structure.text: then may live twice as long as

difficult_points: They 指前句 mice；may 表示可能性。
twice as long as 是倍数比较结构，well-fed 修饰 comrades。

sentences.sentence: Women become distinctly (about 10 per cent) older than men.
sentences.translation: 女性的寿命明显比男性长（约长10%）。

structure.text: become distinctly (about 10 per cent) older than

difficult_points: become older 在本文语境中指活得更久，而非外貌变老。
about 10 per cent 是插入的数量说明，修饰比较程度。

sentences.sentence: If you examine the metabolic rates of the two sexes you establish that the higher male metabolic rate roughly accounts for the lower male life span.
sentences.translation: 如果比较两性的代谢率，就会发现男性较高的代谢率大致解释了男性较短的寿命。

structure.text: If you examine the metabolic rates of the two sexes
that the higher male metabolic rate roughly accounts for the lower male life span

difficult_points: If 从句是一般现在时条件句；you 是泛指“人们”。
account for 表示“解释、占据原因”，that 从句中 higher 与 lower 构成对比。

expressions.text: metabolic rates of the two sexes

sentences.sentence: That means that they live life 'energetically'—more intensively, but not for as long.
sentences.translation: 这意味着它们以更“有活力”的方式生活——生活得更高强度，但持续时间没有那么长。

structure.text: more intensively, but not for as long

difficult_points: means 后接 that 宾语从句，they 指男性。
破折号后用比较方式补充说明 energetically，not for as long 省略了 live。

sentences.sentence: It follows from the above that sparing use of energy reserves should tend to extend life.
sentences.translation: 由以上内容可以推知，节约使用能量储备往往有助于延长生命。

structure.text: that sparing use of energy reserves should tend to extend life

difficult_points: It follows from...that... 是固定结构，表示“由……可推知”。
sparing use 是动名词短语作从句主语，should tend to 表示趋势而非绝对结论。

sentences.sentence: Extreme high-performance sports may lead to optimal cardiovascular performance, but they quite certainly do not prolong life.
sentences.translation: 极限高强度运动可能带来最佳的心血管表现，但肯定不会延长寿命。

structure.text: but they quite certainly do not prolong life

difficult_points: may lead to 表示可能结果，but 后 quite certainly 强调否定判断。
they 指 extreme high-performance sports，prolong life 是“延长寿命”。

sentences.sentence: Relaxation lowers metabolic rate, as does adequate sleep and in general an equable and balanced personality.
sentences.translation: 放松会降低代谢率，充足睡眠以及总体平和、均衡的性格也是如此。

structure.text: as does adequate sleep and in general an equable and balanced personality

difficult_points: as does... 是倒装结构，完整意思是 adequate sleep does so。
and 连接 adequate sleep 与 personality，均表示降低代谢率。

sentences.sentence: Each of us can develop his or her own 'energy-saving programme' with a little self-observation, critical self-control and, above all, logical consistency.
sentences.translation: 我们每个人都可以通过一点自我观察、严格的自我控制，尤其是逻辑上的坚持，制定自己的“节能计划”。

structure.text: with a little self-observation, critical self-control and, above all, logical consistency

difficult_points: Each of us 是单数概念，谓语用 can develop；his or her 与之保持性别包容。
with 短语列出实现计划所需的三项品质，above all 表示最重要的是。

sentences.sentence: Experience will show that to live in this way not only increases the life span but is also very healthy.
sentences.translation: 实践将会证明，以这种方式生活不仅能延长寿命，而且非常健康。

structure.text: that to live in this way not only increases the life span but is also very healthy
not only increases the life span but is also very healthy

difficult_points: to live in this way 是 that 从句的主语，not only...but also... 连接两个谓语。
is also very healthy 的主语仍是 to live in this way，不能理解为 life span 健康。

sentences.sentence: This final aspect should not be forgotten.
sentences.translation: 这一最后的方面不应被忘记。
difficult_points: should not be forgotten 是情态动词加被动语态，表示提醒或建议。
This 指前句所说健康这一附加益处。
